reviews.review_msg: Lectures are good, although there were a few slides that I felt had too much text. Just do and understand the homework, and you should get a good grade.

Gary Parker is an amazing prof but also the material in this course is quite difficult to understand. His lectures were great and he's very good at explaining things. Exams were very fair and just based on the assignments and textbook problems, but can sometimes be a bit tricky. If you take this course, do TONS of practice problems

Caring prof that wants students to do well. His lectures make a lot of sense and hw is helpful (not for marks and he posts the answers). He has clear expectations for whats on the quizzes/exams. He really likes to test your understanding on the exams. But as long as you read the lecture notes, study the tutorials, and do the hw, then youre good

Great guy and does a lot to make students have a better understanding of the concepts. Fair exams since he gives an outline of things he could test on so no surprises but exams can still be tricky. Definitely a better experience than the pre req for this course

Gary is a great and caring professor who genuinely wants his students to succeed. If you complete the homework he assigns, there won't be any surprises on the exams. The course covers a lot of material and is inherently challenging, but Gary does an excellent job making the concepts easy to understand.

I would recommend Dr. Parker to anyone who needs to take Stat 271. He is great at making the course content easier to understand and his exams/quizzes are easy as long as you study. He really cares about his students and does his best to support their learning.
Caring, chill, one of the best math prof at sfu. Great lectures and organization. Fair exams fair grading, the course itself can be difficult.
snarky

His lectures are pretty good; he explains a lot of the intuition behind concepts which helps a lot. Tests are pretty fair since most of the questions are based on the assignments. If you do the assignments then you should do well in the class. Overall he's a pretty chill dude.

Gary Parker is a responsible and nice professor. Time series is a very difficult concept in the statistics industry, but he explained it very well. There are mathematical questions in the course that are tough but very helpful for us to understand the concept. Gary sacrifices his precious time for those students who are struggling with the course.
Lots of theories and calculations, but most of the time you don't understand them. Even though I got a B, I didn't really learn anything
Hard Test, Unclear lectures but a Nice Person

Extremely boring lectures, extremely boring. He spends so much time explaining theoretical materials which is practically useless and most students, like me, forget everything he teaches the day after his final exam. Let's be real, who is going to calculate auto-correlation manually with a pen and paper when everything is done by computer nowadays

Gary's class covers diverse topics, often lacking continuity. Quizzes and midterms are strict, with stringent process requirements and little credit for partial work. Both teaching and grading need refinement. Attendance and proactive homework are crucial for success here."
Hard test. The scoring criteria are extremely unfair. Obvious errors in his notes. The tutorials are useless.

Really good professor. Don't be so confident. This course you must pay a lot of attention in order to get a good grade, even if you are a genius. Don't blame the professor if you did not get a good grade. You get a low grade shows you should not learn actuarial science, or you did not work hard enough.

Gary was the best professor I ever had in university. YES, his course is very, very difficult. But if you put the time in, you can do well. As he once said, "You do you best, and I will do mine." I've never met a prof that cared more about the success and learning of his students.

Gary is one of the best professor I have ever had. The class material is complicated, but he does a great job in teaching and explaining in practical ways. Ask any unclear question because he expects clear understanding. Hard work is necessary, yet it pays off. Overall interesting class Good luck to anyone who will take the class.
Best Instructor in for ACMA in SFU, he is very knowledgeable. Exam are very though, you need to study hard and know everything, but you learn  a lot from this course.

If you want to get into SOA you don't need to take Actuarial Sciences courses in university. You can learn what they teach by taking math and finance courses and they won't hurt your GPA. Gary can't teach wrong prof for this class. Don't get the textbooks use his lecture notes and the internet to find what you need.
Best professor I ever had. Loves to teach, very difficult class but if you put the time in you can do fine. He put it best as "You try your best and I'll try mine."
Very helpful prof. Tests are very hard, but that's what you get if you want to get into ACMA. You need to put in a lot of effort and do a lot of supplementary exercises. Overall, very good prof.
Hardcore class!  Took literally days worth of practicing and studying to simply pass midterms, but it really pays off.  Gary Parker is too funny :p
HARD. That's all there's to it. Be prepared to work your @$$ off in this course. 3.5+? Haha try 4.0+. Gary is a nice guy though.
too hard

This is thee prof you want if you think your GPA is too high and wanna lower it (my GPA is 3.5+ and got an F >:( ). Few examples given in class. Tons of proofs (not practical). MT and Final harder than those questions on SOA. and last assignment DUE AFTER FINAL EXAM (that's something new...)
 
 Yup, great prof....
great prof, tough exam
SUPER Tough... hardest course even in SFU... SUPER tough MTs and Finals, u won't even believe how hard the materials can get!

The best professor I've ever met. However, this course is tough and the exam is really hard coz most students need this course to get admission to the actuarial science. Anyway, if you want to take the SOA exam and want to really know something about theory of interest, take his course.

Never!!!!! take this course unless u really want to get into acma program.got worst mark I never seen in my entire school life.Never believe in his saying " all problems on the test will be from your assignments and practice problems in your book"
No Comments
really helpful prof, great attitude toward students. exams r hard, but reasonable. I m lucky i aint getting lots of his courses. coz it really drag down ur GPA a lot.
He expects you to know the material like the back of your hand but it's really for your own good. Crazy exams, but he's just preparing you for the SOA's.
tough exams and assignments even worse than SOA level,but very helpful lecture teaching. Really a good prof!
